secondary_archetype: Generalist PM — internal platform / ops tooling focus

level_signals: 7+ years of experience building and shipping software products
Staff-level title explicitly stated in role name
Leads complex, cross-functional initiatives as individual contributor
Comp range $155K–$225K base consistent with Staff PM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience building and shipping software products, ideally in SaaS, fintech, or related domains.
Demonstrated track record of taking products or major features from zero to meaningful customer benefit, including defining the problem, validating solutions, and iterating post-launch.
Proven experience leading migrations or systems transformations (for example, claims platforms, workflow tools, or other critical internal/enterprise systems).
Strong customer experience sense, with a demonstrated ability to deeply understand internal workflows while maintaining clear sight of the end-customer experience.
Demonstrated ability to use AI tools as part of daily product work, with a willingness to learn and expand knowledge in this domain.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical and operational concepts to diverse stakeholders and guide discussions to actionable decisions.
Measured success delivering against company and strategic goals, with clear examples of impact on both customer outcomes and business metrics.
Ability to balance short-term execution with long-term platform and experience investments.
Comfort with change and ambiguity; experience operating in rapidly growing and evolving environments.
Strong influence skills and collaborative mindset, with a track record of driving alignment across diverse stakeholders without relying on formal authority.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with the business of insurance or other regulated financial products (underwriting, policy administration, claims, or compliance-heavy workflows).
Experience with enterprise systems used by internal operations teams, such as Salesforce, NetSuite, or similar platforms.
Prior work with Data Science and ML teams, including designing products that use predictive models, risk scores, or automated decisioning while managing operational and regulatory considerations.
Familiarity with cyber risk, security operations, or incident response workflows.

scope.surface: Claims experience platform — internal workflow tooling, automation, ML-powered insights, and broker/policyholder-facing claims experiences
scope.business_model: B2B insurtech / cyber insurance SaaS with internal platform orientation
primary_metrics_inferred: Claims handling speed and resolution time
Manual overhead reduction for claims professionals
Operational efficiency across the claims workflow
Broker and policyholder satisfaction with claims experience
core_responsibilities: Define and evolve the claims experience strategy and product roadmap
Lead complex software migrations and platform evolution end-to-end
Serve internal customers (claims professionals, operations, finance, actuarial) with strong customer experience mindset
Leverage AI and automation across the product lifecycle and partner with engineering and data science
Own requirements, documentation, and cross-functional communication
Make data-informed decisions using quantitative and qualitative data
Lead intricate, cross-functional initiatives spanning multiple teams and systems

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (claims experience platform)
Staff-level individual contributor role with real cross-functional scope
Strong mission context (cyber insurance, protecting businesses from digital risk)
Explicit equal opportunity and accommodations language
red_flags: No equity mentioned — unusual omission at Staff PM level
Wide comp range ($155K–$225K) indicates significant geographic pay differential; actual offer may be at lower end for many US markets
Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international candidates
Role spans many cross-functional stakeholders (Claims, Actuarial, Finance, Legal, Data Science, Ops) which may signal high coordination overhead

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level ('Staff IC' inferred from role title and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as remote-us from 'United States/Canada, with flexible, distributed working arrangements'; Canada eligibility noted but role title/comp stated in US terms)
ambiguities: The JD states the position is 'based in the United States/Canada' — it is unclear whether Canada-based candidates are eligible for the same comp range, which is quoted only in USD. Treated as remote-us primary but Canada may also be eligible.

Equity is not mentioned in the compensation or perks sections, which is atypical for a Staff-level PM role at a Series E+ insurtech; may have been omitted from the JD rather than definitively excluded.
Visa sponsorship is never addressed in the JD; marked as 'unclear' rather than open or no-sponsorship.
